---
title: "PCA"
author: "subhash"
date: "2025-04-29"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# Load PCA results for both datasets
pca_289 <- read.table("/Users/subhashmahamkali/Downloads/289_pca.eigenvec", header=F)
colnames(pca_289) <- c("FID", "IID", paste0("PC", 1:10))

pca_400 <- read.table("/Users/subhashmahamkali/Downloads/400_pca_28m.eigenvec", header=F)
colnames(pca_400) <- c("FID", "IID", paste0("PC", 1:10))

data <- read.csv("/Users/subhashmahamkali/Downloads/tpj15853-sup-0001-files1 (1).csv")
data = data[,c(1,2,5)]
colnames(data) <- c("Pinumber", "cluster","Country")

cluster_names <- c("1" = "durra",
                   "2" = "kafir",
                   "3" = "caudatum",
                   "4" = "milo/durra-bicolor",
                   "5" = "mixed/bicolor",
                   "6" = "guinea")  # change if needed

data <- data %>% mutate(cluster = as.character(cluster), ClusterName = cluster_names[cluster])
data = data[,c(1,4)]

# Read and clean data
data2 <- read_excel("/Users/subhashmahamkali/Downloads/13068_2021_2016_MOESM1_ESM (1).xlsx")
colnames(data2) <- data2[1, ]
data2 <- data2[-1, ]
data2 = data2[, c(1,6)]
data2$Type[data2$Type == "Weedy"] <- "Wild sorghum"
data2 <- data2[data2$Type != "S.propinquum", ]
colnames(data2) <- c("Pinumber", "cluster")

pca_meta_289 <- inner_join(pca_289, data2, by = c("IID" = "Pinumber"))
pca_meta_400 <- inner_join(pca_400, data, by = c("IID" = "Pinumber"))

# For 289 dataset, manually assign ClusterName based on 'cluster' column
data2 <- data2 %>%
  mutate(ClusterName = case_when(
    cluster == "Improved" ~ "Improved",
    cluster == "Landrace" ~ "Landrace",
    cluster == "Wild sorghum" ~ "Wild sorghum",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_  # For any unassigned values, set as NA
  ))

colnames(data) <- c("Pinumber", "cluster")

# For 400 dataset, manually assign ClusterName based on 'cluster' column
data <- data %>%
  mutate(ClusterName = case_when(
    cluster == "durra" ~ "durra",
    cluster == "kafir" ~ "kafir",
    cluster == "caudatum" ~ "caudatum",
    cluster == "milo/durra-bicolor" ~ "milo/durra-bicolor",
    cluster == "mixed/bicolor" ~ "mixed/bicolor",
    cluster == "guinea" ~ "guinea",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_  # For any unassigned values, set as NA
  ))

# Now filter out rows where ClusterName is NA
data2 <- data2 %>% filter(!is.na(ClusterName))
data <- data %>% filter(!is.na(ClusterName))

# Merge PCA data with the updated metadata for both datasets
pca_meta_289 <- inner_join(pca_289, data2, by = c("IID" = "Pinumber"))
pca_meta_400 <- inner_join(pca_400, data, by = c("IID" = "Pinumber"))

# Add a 'Set' column to differentiate the two datasets
pca_meta_289$Set <- "SorGSD"
pca_meta_400$Set <- "SAP"

# Combine both datasets
pca_meta <- bind_rows(pca_meta_289, pca_meta_400)

# Define colors and shapes for plotting
shape_vec <- c("SorGSD" = 17, "SAP" = 16)  # Circle for 289 and Triangle for 400 datasets

color_vec <- c(
  "Improved" = "#E41A1C", "Landrace" = "#377EB8", "Wild sorghum" = "#4DAF4A",  # 3 for 289
  "durra" = "#FF7F00", "kafir" = "#FFFF33", "caudatum" = "#984EA3",  # 6 for 400
  "milo/durra-bicolor" = "#F781BF", "mixed/bicolor" = "#A6A6A6", "guinea" = "#FF5733"  # 6 for 400
)

p <- ggplot(pca_meta, aes(x=PC1, y=PC2, color=ClusterName, shape=factor(Set))) +
  geom_point(size=3) +
  scale_shape_manual(values=shape_vec) +
  scale_color_manual(values=color_vec) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x="PC1", y="PC2", color="Subpopulation", shape="Dataset") +
  theme(    text = element_text(size=14),
    legend.title = element_text(size=14),
    legend.text = element_text(size=12)
  )
ggsave("/Users/subhashmahamkali/Documents/gwas_sap/profiling/graphs/pca.pdf", plot = p, width = 8, height = 6)
```
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(plotly)

# Assuming your PCA data and metadata are already prepared
# Combine both datasets
pca_meta <- bind_rows(pca_meta_289, pca_meta_400)

# Create a 3D PCA plot
p3d <- plot_ly(pca_meta, x = ~PC1, y = ~PC2, z = ~PC3, 
               color = ~ClusterName, 
               colors = color_vec, 
               type = "scatter3d", 
               mode = "markers", 
               symbol = ~Set,  # Set the shapes using the 'Set' column
               markers = list(size = 5)) %>%
  layout(scene = list(
            xaxis = list(title = "PC1"),
            yaxis = list(title = "PC2"),
            zaxis = list(title = "PC3")),
         title = "3D PCA Plot of SorGSD and SAP Datasets",
         legend = list(title = list(text = 'Subpopulation')))

# Show the plot
p3d

# To save the 3D plot as an HTML file
htmlwidgets::saveWidget(p3d, "/Users/subhashmahamkali/Documents/gwas_sap/profiling/graphs/pca_3d.html")
```
